article.description: — Ja Morant made a tiebreaking jumper with 18 seconds left, Jaren Jackson Jr. scored 33 points and the Memphis Grizzlies beat the Minnesota Timberwolves 127-125 on Saturday night in a game that was tight from start to finish.
article.headline: Morant hits tiebreaking shot with 18 seconds left as Grizzlies beat Timberwolves 127-125

images.caption: Rudy Gobert finishes a big alley-oop dunk, but Anthony Edwards is issued a technical foul for flexing on the play.
images.name: Ant gets a tech for flexing during Gobert's big alley-oop jam

article.linkText: Morant hits tiebreaking shot with 18 seconds left as Grizzlies beat Timberwolves 127-125
article.story: MINNEAPOLIS -- — Ja Morant made a tiebreaking jumper with 18 seconds left, Jaren Jackson Jr. scored 33 points and the Memphis Grizzlies beat the Minnesota Timberwolves 127-125 on Saturday night in a game that was tight from start to finish. Morant finished with just 12 points, but he had the tying basket with 54 seconds to go before his 11-footer for the lead. Desmond Bane had 21 points for Memphis, going 4 for 8 from 3-point range. Anthony Edwards missed two 3-pointers in the final 7.3 seconds of a game in which neither team led by more than six and featured 25 lead changes. Donte DiVincenzo scored a season-high 27 points for Minnesota. Jaden McDaniels added 21 and Edwards scored 15 for the Wolves, whose three-game winning streak ended. Takeaways Grizzlies: Rookie Jaylen Wells was once again tasked with defending the opponent's top player, as he guarded Edwards for most of the game. Wells held his own against Edwards and added 13 points on the offensive end. Timberwolves: Saturday was the fourth straight game with a new-look starting lineup that included DiVincenzo starting and veteran Mike Conley coming off the bench. Minnesota is now 3-1 since coach Chris Finch made the lineup switch. Key moment Minnesota had the ball with the game tied at 125 and under a minute to play but Julius Randle turned the ball over. It was the 19th turnover of the game by the Wolves, and perhaps the most costly. Key stat Memphis took 108 shots to Minnesota's 83, thanks in large part to 21 offensive rebounds. Rookie center Zach Edey had seven offensive boards. Up next The Grizzlies visit Houston on Monday while the Timberwolves play at Washington on Monday. ------ AP NBA: https://apnews.com/hub/nba

articles.description: The San Antonio Spurs enter Game 1 of the NBA Finals against the New York Knicks as the favorites to win the NBA championship.
articles.headline: NBA championship and Finals MVP odds: Spurs favored vs. Knicks entering Finals

images.caption: Victor Wembanyama (center left) and the San Antonio Spurs are favored entering the NBA Finals.
images.credit: Jesse D. Garrabrant/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Victor Wembanyama [1296x729]

images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Victor Wembanyama are Nos. 1 and 2 in the Finals MVP odds, respectively.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [1296x729]

images.alt: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander

images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander [608x342]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: Jalen Brunson has the New York Knicks in the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999.
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.caption: The Knicks are looking to end the series against the Cavaliers tonight, as they have the second-shortest title odds and are an overwhelming favorite to win the East.
images.name: Knicks/Cavs [1296x729]

images.alt: Jalen Brunson
images.caption: The Knicks are now heavy favorites to win the Eastern Conference after taking a 3-0 series lead over the Cavs.
images.credit: Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE via Getty Images
images.name: Jalen Brunson [608x342]

images.alt: Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) takes a shot as San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle (5) defends during the first quarter during game two of the western conference finals for the 2026 NBA playoffs at Paycom Center.
images.caption: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander how has even odds to win the Finals MVP.
images.name: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander  [1296x729]

images.caption: VIctor Wembanyama poured in 41 points and grabbed 24 boards in the Spurs' thrilling Game 1 win.
